_environment_note.sandbox_constraint: All 3 fetch methods (Method A timedtext endpoint, Method B 3rd-party wrappers, Method C yt-dlp) failed with HTTP 403 in this sandbox. The managed remote execution environment uses a network-policy allowlist that excludes youtube.com, googleusercontent.com, youtubetranscript.com, youtube-transcript.io, and tactiq.io. Direct curl returns 'Host not in allowlist'.
_environment_note.what_was_tested: Method A: https://www.youtube.com/api/timedtext?lang=en&v=...&fmt=json3 -> 403. Method B: youtubetranscript.com, youtube-transcript.io, tactiq.io -> 403. Method C: yt-dlp -> 403 on YouTube API page download.
_environment_note.what_did_work: raw.githubusercontent.com (in allowlist) — but tech-leader essays not mirrored verbatim there. WebSearch (Anthropic-side service) works for getting AI summaries but not full transcripts.
_environment_note.honest_consequence: Run 4 corpus expansion remains DEFERRED to a non-sandboxed execution. youtube_fetcher_v2.py is correct code — it tries 3 different endpoints with appropriate parsers — but the sandbox blocks all of them. The user's hypothesis ('timedtext endpoint + 3rd-party wrappers bypass IP block') is correct in general but does not work in THIS sandbox.
_environment_note.what_to_do_next: Run `python paradigm_shift/youtube_fetcher_v2.py fetch --seed_path paradigm_shift/youtube_seed_urls.json` from a non-sandboxed machine.
